<recommended_plugins>
Here is a list of plugins that are available but not installed. If the user's query would benefit from one of these plugins, use the `request_plugin_install` tool to suggest that they install it. Pass the parenthesized ID as `plugin_id`. For example, suggest the Google Drive plugin if the query could possibly be better answered with access to Google Drive.

- Atlassian Rovo (atlassian-rovo@openai-curated-remote)
- Box (box@openai-curated-remote)
- Gmail (gmail@openai-curated-remote)
- Google Calendar (google-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Calendar (outlook-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Email (outlook-email@openai-curated-remote)
- SharePoint (sharepoint@openai-curated-remote)
- Teams (teams@openai-curated-remote)
</recommended_plugins>
i want you to go through the codebase with 10 different code-reaper subagents and tell me what we should clean up in this codebase to make it more maintainable.
I’m applying the code-reaper workflow to the architecture/domain-ownership slice. I’ll keep this read-only and verify each recommendation against concrete symbols and call paths.

content.text:
Read-only architecture/domain-boundary audit complete. No P0 findings.

### P1 — Request authorization is owned by HTTP/DB instead of the domain

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:537](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:537) defines request visibility.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:558](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:558) defines the capability matrix.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:594](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:594) defines mergeability.
- Those HTTP helpers make authorization decisions at lines 93, 113, 149, 174, and 299.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/request_access.rs:11](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/request_access.rs:11) separately derives role/audience and edit/maintainer access inside persistence.

Why it is risky: request policy is split across transport, persistence, and transition code. The response DTO itself has become the policy object, so a new delivery mechanism can disagree with HTTP.

Simplest cleanup: create a pure `domain::requests::access` module containing visibility, actor-role/base-audience derivation, capabilities, and mergeability. Make DB authorization and HTTP response mapping consume that one result.

### P1 — `scope-core` domain errors depend directly on Axum/HTTP

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/error.rs:1](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/error.rs:1) imports Axum.
- `ApiError` stores `http::StatusCode` at line 14 and implements `IntoResponse` at line 82.
- Domain modules import it directly, including [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:1](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:1), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:12](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:12), and [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_collaboration.rs:5](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_collaboration.rs:5).
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:7](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:7) only detects direct module-name imports, so this transitive delivery dependency passes.

Why it is risky: domain behavior cannot stand independently of HTTP, and status-code choices are mixed into invariants.

Simplest cleanup: replace `ApiError` in domain code with neutral typed errors/error kinds; implement the HTTP status and `IntoResponse` conversion in `api`. Extend the boundary test to reject delivery dependencies, not only direct module paths.

### P1 — Production exposes an “entire database as `AppCatalog`” escape hatch

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:136](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:136) exposes generic `MetadataStore::read`.
- Its Postgres branch calls `load_catalog` at line 145.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:444](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:444) loads users, every repository, members, invites, cleanup queues, requests, events, accounts, and ledger entries through line 533.
- Outer/application code uses it for narrow reads at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:35](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:35), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:285](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:285), and [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:491](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:491).
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_collaboration.rs:148](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_collaboration.rs:148) even implements token lookup through this full-catalog path.

Why it is risky: callers bypass focused persistence ownership, and adding any catalog field expands the cost/coupling of unrelated reads.

Simplest cleanup: add targeted methods for collaboration views, invite lookup, member/user lookup, and referenced-blob keys; then restrict generic catalog reads to memory/test support.

### P1 — Repository read/discovery policy has two implementations

Evidence:

- Canonical-looking domain logic exists at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:416](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:416).
- Almost identical logic is repeated at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:456](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:456).
- `access_for_principal` at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:448](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:448) is a no-op wrapper that adds an unused `AppState` and fallible return.
- Repository discoverability adds another API-owned rule at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:430](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:430).

Why it is risky: HTTP, Git, and SSE all call the API copy, while tests also exercise the domain copy; either can change independently.

Simplest cleanup: move discoverability/readability onto `StoredRepository` or one domain access service, call it directly everywhere, and delete the `AppState` wrappers.

### P2 — Push lifecycle authorization is duplicated between adapters

Evidence:

- HTTP push-intent creation branches over waiting-first-push, published, and pending-import states at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:163](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:163).
- Git receive-pack repeats the same lifecycle/role decisions at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:312](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:312) and again for user credentials at line 352.

Why it is risky: two delivery adapters own the same repository state machine and concealment semantics.

Simplest cleanup: add one domain push-authorization decision returning a typed outcome such as first push, published push, request-only push, pending review, or denied. HTTP and Git should only translate that outcome.

### P2 — Required change notifications remain manual route choreography

Evidence:

- `RepoEffects` models only storage/blob deletion at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14).
- Notification publication is manually paired with mutations in [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:122](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:122), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:194](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:194), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:482](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:482), and [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:674](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:674).
- Request refreshes use a special zero version at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:37](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:37).

Why it is risky: adding a mutation requires remembering an unrelated post-commit call and reason/version convention.

Simplest cleanup: return a committed mutation receipt/change effect from persistence and dispatch it through one application service after commit. Routes should not construct notification semantics.

### P3 — Two catch-all files are at natural split points

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:190](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:190) combines dependency construction, push-intent cryptography, repo lookup/access, event dispatch, and two cleanup workflows in 731 lines. Split mechanically into `push_intents`, `repo_access`, and `cleanup`; leave `AppState` as composition only.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:19](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:19) is 959 lines and mixes models, credit grants, revisions, editors, withdrawal, timeline actions, resolution, and merge. `submission` and `settlement` are already extracted; finish the same behavior-owned split while keeping facade re-exports.

Strong areas that should not be reaped:

- `StoredRepository::access_for_user_id` at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:283](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:283) is a good singular repository capability matrix.
- `RepoMutation`/`RepoEffects` at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14), persisted atomically through [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_effects.rs:22](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_effects.rs:22), is the right pattern.
- The focused DB module inventory and documented ownership at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:1](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:1) should remain.
- Projection view identity derived from repository access in `domain/projection_views.rs` is appropriately domain-owned.
- The boundary test is worth keeping; strengthen it rather than replacing it.
Read-only audit delivered to the parent agent, with seven ranked findings and five areas to preserve. No files were changed.

No P0 findings. Strongest verified architecture/domain-boundary findings:

### P1 — Request authorization is owned by HTTP and persistence, not the domain

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:537](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:537) defines request visibility.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:558](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:558) defines the capability matrix used for authorization at lines 93, 113, 149, 174, and 299.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:594](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:594) defines mergeability.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/request_access.rs:11](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/request_access.rs:11) separately derives role/audience and edit/maintainer access inside persistence.

Risk: policy is split across transport, persistence, and domain-transition code. The HTTP response DTO effectively becomes a policy object, making other adapters likely to disagree.

Cleanup: create `domain::requests::access` with pure visibility, actor-role/base-audience, capability, and mergeability decisions. DB authorization and HTTP DTO mapping should consume the same domain result.

### P1 — The durable domain is directly coupled to Axum/HTTP errors

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/error.rs:1](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/error.rs:1) imports Axum.
- `ApiError` stores `StatusCode` at line 14 and implements `IntoResponse` at line 82.
- Domain modules import it directly, including [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:1](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:1), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:12](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:12), and [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_collaboration.rs:5](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_collaboration.rs:5).
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:7](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:7) checks only direct forbidden module references, so this transitive delivery dependency passes.

Risk: invariants are expressed in HTTP concepts, preventing the domain from standing independently of delivery.

Cleanup: introduce neutral typed domain errors/error kinds and map them to status codes/`IntoResponse` only in `api`. Strengthen the boundary test to reject delivery dependencies.

### P1 — Production exposes an “entire database as `AppCatalog`” escape hatch

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:136](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:136) exposes generic `MetadataStore::read`.
- Its Postgres branch calls `load_catalog` at line 145.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:444](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:444) loads users, every repository, members, invites, cleanup queues, requests, events, accounts, and ledger entries through line 533.
- Narrow outer-layer reads use it at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:35](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:35), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:285](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:285), and [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:491](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:491).
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_collaboration.rs:148](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_collaboration.rs:148) performs invite-token lookup through this full-catalog path.

Risk: callers bypass focused persistence ownership; unrelated schema additions enlarge and couple every generic read.

Cleanup: add targeted methods for collaboration views, invite lookup, user/member lookup, and referenced-blob keys, then restrict generic catalog reads to memory/test support.

### P1 — Repository read/discovery policy has two implementations

Evidence:

- Domain logic exists at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:416](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:416).
- Almost identical logic is repeated at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:456](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:456).
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:448](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:448) is a no-op `access_for_principal` wrapper that adds an unused `AppState` and fallible return.
- Repository discoverability adds another API-owned rule at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:430](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:430).

Risk: HTTP, Git, and SSE call the API copy while domain tests exercise the core copy; the sources can drift.

Cleanup: put discoverability/readability on `StoredRepository` or one domain access service, call it directly, and delete the `AppState` wrappers.

### P2 — Push lifecycle authorization is duplicated between adapters

Evidence:

- HTTP push-intent creation branches over waiting-first-push, published, and pending-import states at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:163](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:163).
- Git receive-pack repeats these lifecycle/role decisions at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:312](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:312) and again for user credentials at line 352.

Risk: two delivery adapters own the same repository state machine and concealment rules.

Cleanup: add one domain push-authorization decision returning a typed outcome such as first push, published push, request-only push, pending review, or denied. Adapters should only translate the outcome.

### P2 — Change notifications remain manual route choreography

Evidence:

- `RepoEffects` models only storage/blob deletion at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14).
- Notification publication is manually paired with mutations at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:122](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repos.rs:122), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:194](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/repo_collaboration.rs:194), [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:482](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:482), and [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:674](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:674).
- Request refreshes use a special zero version at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:37](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:37).

Risk: every new mutation must remember a separate post-commit call and reason/version convention.

Cleanup: return a committed mutation receipt/change effect and dispatch notifications through one application service after commit. Routes should not construct notification semantics.

### P3 — Two catch-all files are at natural behavior-owned split points

Evidence:

- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:190](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:190) combines dependency construction, push-intent cryptography, repo lookup/access, event dispatch, and cleanup workflows in 731 lines.
- [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:19](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests.rs:19) is 959 lines and mixes models, credits, revisions, editors, withdrawal, timeline actions, resolution, and merge.

Risk: ownership is broad and unrelated changes collide. `requests.rs` is already partially split into `submission` and `settlement`, showing the intended direction.

Cleanup: mechanically split `state.rs` into `push_intents`, `repo_access`, and `cleanup`, leaving `AppState` as composition. Finish splitting requests into model, collaboration, and resolution/lifecycle modules behind facade re-exports.

Strong areas that should not be reaped:

- `StoredRepository::access_for_user_id` at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:283](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:283) is a good singular repository capability matrix.
- `RepoMutation`/`RepoEffects` at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:14), persisted through [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_effects.rs:22](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_effects.rs:22), is the right explicit-side-effect pattern.
- The focused DB module inventory and documented ownership at [/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:1](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:1) should remain.
- Projection view identity derived from repository access in `domain/projection_views.rs` is appropriately domain-owned.
- The domain-boundary test should be strengthened, not removed.

No files were changed.
